person.years_as_billionaire_note: Becoming the primary controller in 2021 upon Sheldon's death. Household had been billionaire-level since 1990s, but Miriam as record-holder is ~5 years.

Dallas Mavericks equity (~$3.5B share, family 69% of $3.5B club val) adds illiquid component.

We include this as pre-2018 lifetime floor to acknowledge giving history, but only count the 2018-2024 990 numbers in the observable rollup to avoid double-counting.

pledges_and_announcements.notes: Confirmed: Neither Miriam Adelson nor (posthumously) Sheldon Adelson appear on the Giving Pledge signatory list. Despite giving >$1B cumulatively over their lifetimes, the Adelsons never signed the Pledge. Miriam's public framing emphasizes Israel/Jewish causes and political influence rather than the 50%-of-wealth Pledge commitment.

pledges_and_announcements.note: Wikipedia/Chronicle of Philanthropy cite Adelson Foundations giving '$200 million annually.' ACTUAL 990-PF data shows AFF + AMRF combined grants_out closer to $80-150M/year (peaked $145M in 2020, declined to $109M in 2024). The $200M claim is either aspirational/historic-peak framing or includes non-tax-deductible direct gifts outside the foundations. Flag as marketing-claim-not-matching-filings.

pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: Trump told White House Hanukkah attendees Miriam gave '$250 million indirectly and directly' to his campaign. Publicly verified figures are $106M to Preserve America PAC; the $250M figure reflects Trump's own stated total including indirect channels not in FEC records. Not counted as philanthropy in any case.

political_giving.substitution_note: Political dominates charitable by an extreme margin. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Miriam Adelson is among the most extreme examples in the Forbes 400 of political-giving dominance relative to personal philanthropy.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Floor from press/FEC sources. Includes: $106M to Preserve America PAC in 2024 cycle; $40M split between Senate Leadership Fund ($30M) and Congressional Leadership Fund ($10M) in 2022 cycle (per The Hill / FEC); $10M at pledge amount to Preserve America in early 2024 (seed). 2021/2023 were off-cycle.
political_giving.observed_total_lifetime_note: Per Wikipedia citing May 2024 OpenSecrets: Miriam's lifetime political donations were $284M, exceeding her late husband's $273M. Combined household lifetime ~$557M+ in political giving — dwarfs their combined charitable ~$1-1.5B lifetime estimate and makes them among the largest donors in American history.

red_flags.evidence: $106M to single super-PAC (Preserve America) in 2024 cycle alone vs ~$109M total AFF+AMRF charitable grants 2024. Lifetime political $284M (Miriam alone) exceeds household charitable flows through the two foundations in any comparable window. 2022 midterms: $40M political vs ~$81M charitable. 2026 midterms: $40M political already committed April 2026. Political is not intermittent — it is structural.

Neither Miriam nor Sheldon Adelson ever signed the Giving Pledge despite decades as top-20-US billionaires. Given ~$35B net worth, 50% Pledge would be ~$17.5B target — foundations' lifetime giving is far below that threshold.

AFF grants_out: $117M (2019) → $99M (2020) → $79M (2021) → $45M (2022) → $44M (2023) → $67M (2024). Peak charitable giving was pre-Sheldon-death; post-inheritance, charitable giving cut roughly in half while political giving scaled up. Pattern is consistent with substitution (political displacing charitable), not with wealth-increase-driving-giving-up.

Adelson Foundations publicly claim '$200M annually' to Jewish/Israeli causes. Actual AFF+AMRF 990-PF grants_out combined: 2024=$109M, 2023=$81M, 2022=$81M, 2021=$117M, 2020=$152M, 2019=$145M. Only 2019-2020 approach $150M; claim of $200M/yr appears inflated by ~30-50%.

Adelson political giving correlates with business-policy wins (online gaming regulation, Macau relations, Israel policy supporting West Bank expansion where Ariel U. is located). Political-plus-charitable bundling with Israel advocacy means some 'charitable' giving (e.g., Ariel University, IAC, Israel Hayom-adjacent NGOs) operates as political influence by other means. Methodology note: does not change observable-giving dollar figures but informs qualitative read.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (both foundations operate as pass-through — grants > assets by multiples)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (AMRF staff paid modestly; trustees including Miriam take $0)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified, but Schedule B parse not yet run — absence-of-evidence only)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(AFF 990-PF 2018-2024 grants_out) + sum(AMRF 990-PF 2018-2024 grants_out). AFF 2018-2024 = $568,762,974. AMRF 2018-2024 = $270,403,135. Total = $839,166,109. Excludes pre-2018 lifetime press totals (dedup risk); excludes Dallas Mavericks (not philanthropy); excludes political giving; excludes Trump-claimed $250M indirect (not philanthropy anyway).

Parse Schedule B on latest 990 filings to verify or reject DAF_TRANSFERS flag.

rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.35 × $35B = $12.25B; tenure = 5 years post-Sheldon → 0.5 multiplier. 10% × $12.25B × 0.5 = $612.5M. NOTE: if you count household-tenure (since 1990s), multiplier saturates and expected = $1.225B. Using individual-as-record-holder tenure for consistency with Ellison methodology.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Schedule B not yet parsed.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Miriam's public stance explicitly REJECTS anonymous giving ('If I gave my name, others would donate — so we surrendered' — eJewishPhilanthropy). Her model is public/named giving to catalyze matching. Anonymous uplift set to zero.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Religious giving to synagogues, Israel-based Jewish orgs, and Chabad is well-documented in Adelson coverage. Sheldon was quietly generous to individual Chabad requests (per Chabad.org obituary). Apply 0.5% of liquid wealth as cohort-upper for observant-Jewish household giving not captured in 501(c)(3) filings. 0.005 × $12.25B = $61M/yr; over 5-year Miriam window = $300M. Settle at $175M as conservative upper given much of Jewish giving DOES flow through AFF and is thus already counted.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Sheldon's estate / Miriam's estate trust structure is not publicly disclosed. Upper-bound charitable outcome via charitable remainder provisions could be ~50% of liquid wealth per Pledge-equivalent, but no such pledge exists. Conservative envelope: $1.5B uncertainty band reflecting possibility of eventual estate disposition, without evidence.

rollup.tier_rule: Observable giving ($839M) exceeds strict expected ($613M), which on face would suggest NOT low. HOWEVER, the key Scrooge-list interpretive question is: does political-substitution-dominance + no-pledge-signature + declining-post-Sheldon-trajectory justify flagging? YES. Ratio of political giving to charitable giving is approximately 1.0-1.2x in the measurement window (political slightly exceeds charitable for 2024 single-cycle: $106M political vs $109M charitable). The POLITICAL_HEAVY classification overrides raw observable-ratio for tier placement. Under pure-dollar observable ratio she'd be Tier C (active giver); under political-substitution-adjusted ratio she's Tier B (Probably Low relative to capacity given political displacement).
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Miriam Adelson presents the canonical POLITICAL_SUBSTITUTION case. Observable charitable giving is roughly at or slightly above the strict 10% norm, BUT she simultaneously deploys equal or greater dollars on partisan political influence which under NBER W26616 displaces ~33 cents of charitable per dollar political. For readers: her ~$840M observable charitable would likely be ~$890M+ absent the ~$156M+ recent political giving. The Giving Pledge absence despite decades as top-50-billionaire household is the stronger critique than the observable-ratio itself.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Component breakdown: no giving gap (observable > expected on strict formula), moderate tenure penalty (5 years as record-holder post-Sheldon), Pledge penalty LOW (never signed, so can't 'breach' — downgraded to 12 for capacity-given-no-pledge), red flags maxed (5 flags × 5pts, capped at 20). DO NOT PUBLISH — tiers only.
